classDescription: A wilderness hunter and skirmisher with primal magic.
roleIdentity: Mobile striker and scout with sustained utility and control tools.

ranger-favored-enemy.summary: Hunter's Mark is always prepared, costs no spell slot to cast (twice per Long Rest), and deals +1d6 bonus damage to your marked target on every weapon hit.
ranger-favored-enemy.description: You always have the Hunter's Mark spell prepared; it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare. You can cast Hunter's Mark twice without expending a spell slot. You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. Hunter's Mark (Concentration, up to 1 hour, range 90 ft): you curse one creature you can see. You deal an extra 1d6 damage to that creature whenever you hit it with a weapon attack. If the target dies before the spell ends, you can use a Bonus Action to move the mark to a new creature within range.
ranger-favored-enemy.effect: Cast Hunter's Mark twice per Long Rest without expending a spell slot. Marked target takes +1d6 damage from your weapon attacks. On target death, use Bonus Action to re-mark a new creature within 90 ft.

ranger-weapon-mastery.summary: You can use the Mastery property of 2 weapon types you are proficient with. You can swap your choices whenever you finish a Long Rest.
ranger-weapon-mastery.description: Your training with weapons allows you to use the Mastery property of two kinds of weapons with which you have proficiency, such as Longbows and Shortswords. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change the weapon types you chose. Each weapon's Mastery property is listed on the weapon's entry in the equipment tables.
ranger-weapon-mastery.effect: Use the Mastery property of 2 proficient weapon types. Swap choices on each Long Rest.

ranger-deft-explorer.summary: Gain Expertise in one skill, +10 ft Speed (not in Heavy Armor), and you ignore Difficult Terrain.
ranger-deft-explorer.description: You are a master of wilderness travel. You gain the following benefits: Expertise — choose one skill you are proficient with; your proficiency bonus is doubled for ability checks using that skill. Roving Speed — your Speed increases by 10 feet while you are not wearing Heavy Armor. Tireless Traveler — you ignore Difficult Terrain.

ranger-spellcasting.summary: Wisdom-based half-caster. Prepare spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + half your Ranger level (rounded down). Spell slots per the half-caster table.
ranger-spellcasting.description: You have learned to cast spells through studying the natural world. Spellcasting Ability: Wisdom. Spell Save DC = 8 + proficiency bonus + Wis modifier. Spell Attack Modifier = proficiency bonus + Wis modifier. Preparing Spells: you prepare a number of Ranger spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + half your Ranger level (rounded down), minimum 1. You can change your prepared spells whenever you finish a Long Rest. Spell Slots: you gain spell slots as shown on the Ranger Spell Slots table; slots begin at 1st level at Ranger level 2.
ranger-spellcasting.effect: Prepare Wis mod + half Ranger level (round down) spells. Cast using Wisdom. Spell slots follow the half-caster progression starting at Ranger level 2.

ranger-fighting-style.summary: Choose one permanent fighting posture: Archery (+2 ranged attack rolls), Defense (+1 AC in armor), Dueling (+2 melee damage one-handed), or Two-Weapon Fighting (add ability mod to off-hand damage).
ranger-fighting-style.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again. Archery: +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons. Defense: +1 bonus to AC while you are wearing armor. Dueling: +2 bonus to damage rolls when you are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons. Two-Weapon Fighting: when you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack.

choices.name: Archery
choices.description: +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with ranged weapons.

choices.name: Defense
choices.description: +1 AC while you are wearing armor.

choices.name: Dueling
choices.description: +2 damage when fighting with a single one-handed melee weapon.

choices.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
choices.description: Add your ability modifier to the damage of your off-hand attack.

choices.name: Blind Fighting
choices.description: Gain blindsight in a short range to fight through darkness or concealment.

choices.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
choices.description: Draw thrown weapons freely and improve thrown-weapon damage.

ranger-conclave-subclass.summary: Choose your Ranger Conclave at level 3. Your conclave grants features at levels 3, 7, 11, and 15.
ranger-conclave-subclass.description: At 3rd level, you choose a Ranger Conclave: the archetype that defines your hunting specialty, such as Beast Master, Gloom Stalker, or Hunter. Your choice grants you features at Ranger levels 3, 7, 11, and 15.

ranger-roving.summary: Your Speed increases by 10 ft, and you gain a Climb Speed and Swim Speed each equal to your Speed.
ranger-roving.description: Your Speed increases by 10 feet. You also gain a Climb Speed equal to your Speed and a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

ranger-tireless.summary: As a Magic action, gain 1d8 + Wis mod Temporary HP (usable Wis mod times per Long Rest). Short Rests also reduce your Exhaustion level by 1.
ranger-tireless.description: Primal forces sustain you through exhausting hunts. You gain two benefits: Temporary Hit Points — as a Magic action, you can give yourself a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d8 plus your Wisdom modifier. You can use this action a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. Shake it Off — whenever you finish a Short Rest, your Exhaustion level, if any, decreases by 1.

ranger-natures-veil.summary: Bonus Action: become Invisible until the start of your next turn. Usable a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier per Long Rest.
ranger-natures-veil.description: You draw on primal magic to hide yourself from view. As a Bonus Action, you magically become Invisible until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

ranger-foe-slayer.summary: Once per turn, add your Wisdom modifier to an attack roll or saving throw d20 roll involving your Hunter's Mark target.
ranger-foe-slayer.description: You become an unparalleled hunter. Once per turn, when the creature marked by your Hunter's Mark makes a saving throw or when you hit that creature with an attack roll, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the d20 roll.

ranger-asi-4.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each, or take a feat. No score can exceed 20.
ranger-asi-4.description: You gain an Ability Score Improvement. You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Alternatively, you can forgo the increase to take a feat for which you qualify.

ranger-extra-attack.summary: You can attack twice, instead of once, when you take the Attack action on your turn.
ranger-extra-attack.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

ranger-subclass-feature-6.summary: Your Ranger Conclave grants an additional feature at level 6 that expands your subclass toolkit.
ranger-subclass-feature-6.description: At 6th level, your chosen Ranger Conclave grants you an additional feature. The specific ability depends on your conclave selection. Refer to your Ranger Conclave's level 6 entry for the full rules.

ranger-expertise-second-skill.summary: Gain Expertise in 2 skills you are proficient with: your proficiency bonus is doubled for those skills.
ranger-expertise-second-skill.description: At 9th level, you gain Expertise in two more skills of your choice in which you are proficient. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen skills.

ranger-conjure-barrage.summary: Once per Long Rest: throw a weapon or fire a piece of ammunition to create a 60-ft cone of spectral copies; each creature in the cone takes 3d8 damage (Con save for half).
ranger-conjure-barrage.description: You use a piece of ammunition or a thrown weapon to create a spray of spectral copies. When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with this feature. Each creature of your choice in a 60-foot cone must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or take 3d8 damage of the same type as the weapon or ammunition used. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. You can use this feature once, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest.

ranger-asi-8.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each, or take a feat. No score can exceed 20.
ranger-asi-8.description: You gain an Ability Score Improvement. You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Alternatively, you can forgo the increase to take a feat for which you qualify.

ranger-lands-stride.summary: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs no extra movement. You have Advantage on saves against magical plants that restrain or slow movement.
ranger-lands-stride.description: Moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard. In addition, you have Advantage on saving throws against plants that are magically created or manipulated to impede movement, such as the area created by the Entangle spell.

ranger-third-level-slots.summary: You gain access to 3rd-level spell slots: 2 slots at Ranger level 9, increasing to 3 slots at level 11.
ranger-third-level-slots.description: At 9th level, your spellcasting advances to include 3rd-level spell slots (2 slots). This allows you to cast any Ranger spell of 3rd level or lower that you have prepared. The number of 3rd-level slots increases to 3 at Ranger level 11.

ranger-subclass-feature-10.summary: Your Ranger Conclave grants an additional feature at level 10 that further defines your hunting specialty.
ranger-subclass-feature-10.description: At 10th level, your chosen Ranger Conclave grants you an additional feature. The specific ability depends on your conclave selection. Refer to your Ranger Conclave's level 10 entry for the full rules.

ranger-conjure-volley.summary: Once per Long Rest: fire a piece of ammunition or throw a weapon to fill a 40-ft radius, 20-ft tall cylinder; creatures inside take 8d8 damage (Dex save for half).
ranger-conjure-volley.description: You fire a piece of ammunition from a ranged weapon or throw a weapon at a point you can see within 600 feet. A volley of spectral copies rains down on the point. Each creature of your choice in a 40-foot radius, 20-foot-tall cylinder centered on the point must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or take 8d8 damage of the same type as the weapon or ammunition used. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. You can use this feature once, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a Long Rest.

ranger-asi-12.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each, or take a feat. No score can exceed 20.
ranger-asi-12.description: You gain an Ability Score Improvement. You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Alternatively, you can forgo the increase to take a feat for which you qualify.

ranger-fourth-level-slots.summary: You gain access to 4th-level spell slots: 1 slot at Ranger level 13, increasing to 2 slots at level 15, then 3 slots at level 17.
ranger-fourth-level-slots.description: At 13th level, your spellcasting advances to include 4th-level spell slots (1 slot). This allows you to cast any Ranger spell of 4th level or lower that you have prepared. The number of 4th-level slots increases to 2 at Ranger level 15, and to 3 at Ranger level 17.

ranger-subclass-feature-14.summary: Your Ranger Conclave grants a high-tier feature at level 14 that defines your late-game capabilities.
ranger-subclass-feature-14.description: At 14th level, your chosen Ranger Conclave grants you an additional powerful feature. The specific ability depends on your conclave selection. Refer to your Ranger Conclave's level 14 entry for the full rules.

ranger-conjure-barrage-improvement.summary: Your Conjure Barrage improves: you can now use it twice per Long Rest, and the damage increases to 5d8.
ranger-conjure-barrage-improvement.description: At 15th level, your Conjure Barrage improves. You can use it twice between Long Rests (instead of once), and the damage it deals increases to 5d8.

ranger-asi-16.summary: Increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1 each, or take a feat. No score can exceed 20.
ranger-asi-16.description: You gain an Ability Score Improvement. You can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1 each. You can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. Alternatively, you can forgo the increase to take a feat for which you qualify.

ranger-fifth-level-slots.summary: You gain access to 5th-level spell slots: 1 slot at Ranger level 17, increasing to 2 slots at level 19.
ranger-fifth-level-slots.description: At 17th level, your spellcasting advances to include 5th-level spell slots (1 slot), completing the Ranger half-caster spell progression. This allows you to cast any Ranger spell of 5th level or lower that you have prepared. The number of 5th-level slots increases to 2 at Ranger level 19.

ranger-feral-senses.summary: You don't have Disadvantage on attacks against Invisible or hidden creatures, as long as you can hear them and aren't Blinded or Deafened.
ranger-feral-senses.description: At 18th level, you gain primal senses that make you difficult to deceive. When you attack a creature you can't see, your inability to see the creature doesn't impose Disadvantage on your attack rolls against it, provided the creature isn't hidden from you and you can hear it.

ranger-epic-boon.summary: At level 19, instead of a standard ASI you gain an Epic Boon feat — a powerful endgame enhancement that can push an ability score above 20.
ranger-epic-boon.description: At 19th level, you gain an Epic Boon feat (see the Epic Boons chapter) or another feat of your choice for which you qualify. This replaces the standard Ability Score Improvement and may increase an ability score above the normal cap of 20.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your weapon accuracy, damage, and stealth options
buildTips.secondaryReason: improves key ranger spells and class utilities
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: you still need staying power and better Concentration checks
